title: Lesson 346
transcript: Music playing All right, we're still in domain number eight and one of the specific items that exists in the exam outline and that people have been seeing lately questions on is what's called canary testing or canary deployment. and you might recognize the word canary here and the reference is in how canaries, the birds, are used or used to be used in coal mines, right? So you've probably read about this but you know miners would walk around certain areas of the coal mine with canaries in a cage to see whether there was toxic air in those areas of the mine because if the poor canaries started to you know, struggle with breathing, then obviously the coal miner would know that that area of the mine was having some toxic air and needed to get out of there quickly. That's where the reference comes from. Because if you think about that, the whole purpose that canaries were used in coal mines is for early warning. So that obviously we protected the safety and life of people. That's the same concept that this addresses in canary deployment and canary testing. And so the idea in software development and application security is that, for example, if you're making a change to something, you know, to the functionality of an application, for example, what you do is you make that change, but you only subject a very small user population to it and you watch what happens. and then you react accordingly. If everything goes good, then you would roll out that change to a larger user population, right? So the whole idea behind this is to provide early warning. And there's a whole bunch of companies that are really doing this in a great way. Tesla comes to mind, for example, when they roll out software for their cars. They only subject a certain number of cars to a specific version of that change, for example, and they watch. And then they'll make modifications and issue a new release to maybe a different user population, for example. So that's the whole idea behind canary deployment. So that once we understand that everything is good, we can start rolling out that change gradually to all users. And it's meant for obviously early warning.
